calibration_88_tokens.killed_by: at 88 tokens the median routing split is only 63.6 percent stable and 26.1 percent of cells never route at all
calibration_88_tokens.verdict: the lever is alive, the 88-token calibration is dead; needs ~1000 tokens

cross_expert_and_cross_layer_tying.instrument_validated: the metric carries a positive control proving it WOULD detect norm-swamping (synthetic pair sharing one 1e4-scale row reads 0.9+ raw, <0.3 normalized), so this is a real measurement rather than a blind instrument
cross_expert_and_cross_layer_tying.killed_by: raw and row-normalized mean |off-diagonal cosine| agree to 3 significant figures (gate 0.00166 vs 0.00168; down 0.00039 both). The 15-decade row-norm span was NOT what killed the raw-space cosine test - the experts are genuinely mutually orthogonal in both spaces. Best single shared template explains 0.2513 of 4 experts' energy against an orthogonal null of exactly 0.2500. Same-expert-index cross-layer tying is indistinguishable from a different-index control at 1e-7.
cross_expert_and_cross_layer_tying.lever: expert templates + deltas in row-normalized space; same-expert-index cross-layer tying
cross_expert_and_cross_layer_tying.reopen_condition: a parent measures row-normalized mean off-diagonal expert cosine >= 0.10
cross_expert_and_cross_layer_tying.verdict: DEAD, and the row-norm rescue hypothesis is falsified.

entropy_coded_pq_indices.killed_by: measured 0.0 to 0.7 percent, not the hoped 10 to 25 percent; Lloyd-optimal indices are near-uniform by construction
entropy_coded_pq_indices.lever: entropy coding of trained PQ indices
entropy_coded_pq_indices.reopen_condition: a future parent uses NON-Lloyd (biased or stratified) codebooks whose measured index entropy is <= 0.9 of uniform
entropy_coded_pq_indices.verdict: the gain is inside the noise of the byte plan

expert_merging_omitted_from_survivors.adversary_note: the verifier found in-sample contamination in the per-expert medians (singleton-hit experts fit and scored on the same token). The contamination runs IN THE MERGE'S FAVOUR, so the true wall is at least this hard. Verdict strengthened, not weakened.
expert_merging_omitted_from_survivors.killed_by: median held-out relative error of the BEST single surviving expert is 0.885/0.993/0.995 at L0/L3/L7; a 4-survivor least-squares merge only reaches 0.863/0.988/0.995. ~1.0 means the reconstruction is no better than predicting zero. Same wall as inter_expert_redundancy: there is no low-dimensional surviving-expert subspace containing a cold expert's function.
expert_merging_omitted_from_survivors.lever: reconstruct an omitted MoE expert from a learned combination of surviving experts
expert_merging_omitted_from_survivors.reopen_condition: a parent measures best-single-survivor reconstruction error <= 0.5 on held-out routed tokens at its own keep fraction

inter_expert_redundancy.killed_by: mean pairwise cosine between experts = 1e-4; experts are mutually orthogonal
inter_expert_redundancy.lever: delta coding / shared low-rank bases / cluster-mean subtraction across experts
inter_expert_redundancy.reopen_condition: a future parent measures mean pairwise expert cosine >= 0.10 on its own weights
inter_expert_redundancy.verdict: dead on arrival: there is no shared component to subtract

kronecker_factorisation.killed_by: at depth the Van Loan rearrangement spectrum is nearly flat - the top Kronecker component holds 0.27 percent of gate energy - so the rank the 2.5 bpw budget buys (191 of 2048) captures only 27.2 percent of energy: rel_error 0.853 vs the incumbent 0.239 at the same rung.
kronecker_factorisation.lever: Kronecker / tensor-product factorisation W ~ A (x) B of a single expert tensor
kronecker_factorisation.reopen_condition: any layer whose Van Loan spectrum is not flat; check before assuming depth behaviour transfers to the first block
kronecker_factorisation.the_exception: layer 0 gate_proj: 0.0301 vs the incumbent codec's 0.2252, at 2.487061 vs 2.500735 complete BPW - a 7.5x error reduction at a CHEAPER rate. The lane skipped layer 0 under an exemption written for cross-expert TYING methods, which does not apply to a single-tensor factorisation.
kronecker_factorisation.verdict: DEAD for layers >= 1 at the S64 rungs; LIVE and codec-beating on layer 0. The lane's original 'structurally dead, nothing here should be built on' was REFUTED by its verifier.

large_expert_cache.killed_by: 0 evictions at a 64GB cap; RAM 70->18GB free and swap down to 906MB free. A single lockstep pass has zero cross-layer reuse
large_expert_cache.lever: aggressive expert cache (64GB cap) for a single lockstep pass
large_expert_cache.reopen_condition: a future parent's schedule has MEASURED cross-layer expert reuse (cache hit rate > 0) instead of a single lockstep pass
large_expert_cache.verdict: correct cap is ~20GB; aggressive RAM only where real reuse exists

layer_zero_is_a_different_source.finding: layer 0 is not a hard cell, it is a DIFFERENT SOURCE, and the codec that is near-optimal for layers >= 1 is badly mismatched to it. Converging evidence: (a) Shannon-bound probe: L0 down is 3.014 bits non-Gaussian with 1.328 decades of unexploited gap, vs 0.012-0.018 bits and 0.06 decades at L46/L93. (b) Column-scale modulation removes the non-Gaussianity completely (3.0143 -> -0.0035 bits) and closes 42 percent of the distortion gap. (c) Activation-aware coding cuts L0 gate held-out output error ~60 percent. (d) Kronecker factorisation, declared structurally dead at depth, BEATS the incumbent codec on L0 gate: 0.0301 vs 0.2252 rel_error at a CHEAPER complete rate (2.487 vs 2.501 BPW). Error rises monotonically with depth: L0 0.0301, L1 0.6611, L2 0.7567, L46 0.85+.
layer_zero_is_a_different_source.killed_by: nothing - this is a POSITIVE entry recording where the campaign found real headroom.
layer_zero_is_a_different_source.lever: treating all 94 layers as one coding problem
layer_zero_is_a_different_source.reopen_condition: N/A - live. The scope is bounded: layer 0 is 1/94 of the model, so exploiting it fully is worth about 1 percent of total bits. Its value is as a DIAGNOSTIC that the source is layer-dependent and a per-layer codec selector is warranted.
layer_zero_is_a_different_source.the_predictor: post_attention_layernorm gamma^2 anisotropy (decades p99/p50) predicts where function-aware coding pays, and it is DATA-FREE: L0 1.8428, L1 0.9035, L2 0.5540, L70 0.4576, L23 0.3112, L46 0.2026, L93 0.0621. A data-free h = gamma^2 recovers 83 percent of the gain that a full routed-token calibration pipeline buys (log-corr(h, gamma^2) = 0.9918 at layer 0).
layer_zero_is_a_different_source.verdict: LIVE AND CONVERGENT. Three independent lanes found the same thing without coordination.

post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.killed_by: Across mid and late layers the coded-space weights ARE essentially Gaussian (non-Gaussianity 0.012-0.018 bits), so there the original conclusion survives: down_proj sits 0.059 decades from its true Shannon bound (measured 0.7026 vs 0.6568) and gate 0.27-0.28 decades (0.2427 vs 0.1749). Best possible post-hoc gain on gate is a factor of ~1.4 in rel_error.
post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.lever: any post-hoc fixed-weight codec improvement at a fixed sub-bit index rate
post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.named_exception: LAYER 0 IS NOT EXHAUSTED. Its down_proj measures 3.014 bits of non-Gaussianity and sits 1.328 decades from its Shannon bound (0.3701 measured vs 0.0803 achievable); layer 0 gate is 0.669 bits non-Gaussian and 0.791 decades off. An organ- and layer-specific codec for the first block is a live, named, unexploited lever - do not transfer 'Lane A is closed' to it.
post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.reopen_condition: any cell measuring >= 0.5 decades of gap to its OWN Shannon lower bound, which is a measurement every future parent must now make rather than assuming the Gaussian number
post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.the_overclaim_and_its_correction: This campaign first sealed Lane A on the grounds that the codec matched sqrt(2^-2R). That reasoning was WRONG: sqrt(2^-2R) is the GAUSSIAN rate-distortion function, and among sources of equal variance the Gaussian MAXIMIZES differential entropy, so its D(R) is an UPPER bound on the distortion any same-variance source requires. Matching it proves the codec is as good as if the weights were Gaussian; it proves nothing about the true floor. The correct floor is the Shannon lower bound D(R) >= (1/2 pi e) 2^(2h) 2^(-2R) using the MEASURED differential entropy h. Re-measured with a Kozachenko-Leonenko estimator (validated to 0.011 bits against the analytic Gaussian entropy) on the real weights in the coded space.
post_hoc_coding_of_frozen_weights.verdict: NEARLY exhausted, NOT closed. CORRECTED from an earlier overclaim in this same campaign.

posthoc_scalar_gain.killed_by: optimal gain pinned at exactly 1.0; k-means reconstruction is a conditional mean so the residual is orthogonal to the reconstruction, and cosine is gain-invariant
posthoc_scalar_gain.lever: post-hoc scalar gain correction on a PQ artifact
posthoc_scalar_gain.reopen_condition: a future parent uses a NON-conditional-mean quantizer whose residual is measurably non-orthogonal to the reconstruction

raw_weight_pq_vq_at_one_bit.killed_by: qwen3-235b:F1 real forward, 6 prompts, 94 layers, healthy parent control (ppl 1.61 to 39.33): A1_1p0 at complete 1.0075 BPW collapsed 6/6 (symKL 7.6 to 10.9, argmax agreement 0.0) and R2_subhalf at complete 0.4930 BPW collapsed 6/6 (symKL 9.3 to 13.5, argmax agreement 0.0)
raw_weight_pq_vq_at_one_bit.lever: PQ/VQ coding of the RAW weights as the route to one bit or below
raw_weight_pq_vq_at_one_bit.reopen_condition: never on raw weights. A method that changes the source is not this lever and is not blocked by this entry
raw_weight_pq_vq_at_one_bit.verdict: the raw-weight PQ/VQ family is dead at about one bit. This kills THAT FAMILY, not the sub-bit program: the correct response is a method that CHANGES the source (QAT, distillation, compressibility training, structured pruning, learned sharing), never a higher rate

row_norm_stratification_premise.killed_by: the PREMISE does not reproduce. The sealed diagnosis claimed 94 percent of gate/up rows collapse onto ONE codeword. Measured on real weights at the exact R2 geometry (d16 k1024, 5 layers x 3 organs x 8 experts): single-codeword share is 0.0267 mean and never exceeds 0.047 in any cell. The 94 percent figure belonged to a different (k=32) geometry and does not transfer.
row_norm_stratification_premise.reopen_condition: a parent measures single-codeword share >= 0.20 at its own deployed geometry
row_norm_stratification_premise.verdict: the stated pathology is absent; stratification has nothing to fix. Its STRONGER form (per-row scale normalization, M01') was built and does help, but only 2.8 percent mean.

ternary_factorization.killed_by: loses to VQ at matched rate
ternary_factorization.reopen_condition: a future parent diagnoses a weight distribution where ternary beats VQ at a matched exact rate on a real forward
ternary_factorization.verdict: dominated at every matched rate tested

uniform_subbit_allocation.killed_by: F0 real forward: both the uniform and the treated artifact collapsed
uniform_subbit_allocation.lever: uniform sub-bit allocation across organs (and its treated variant)
uniform_subbit_allocation.reopen_condition: a future parent measures NO organ sensitivity split (gate and down degrade within 10 percent of each other)
uniform_subbit_allocation.verdict: superseded by organ inversion (mlp1 gate+up sensitive, mlp2 down tolerant), confirmed by F1 qwen3-235b dominant_failure_organ = gate
